description: Sonic lineage reference library. Each entry traces a tradition, its ancestors, and what it carries culturally and sonically.

lineages.name: Delta Blues

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: raw longing, unmediated grief, direct address

lineages.cultural_context: Mississippi Delta, Great Migration era. Expression of survival and witness under systemic oppression. Technique as shared community property — guitar passed between hands, patterns learned by watching.
lineages.kindpath_notes: The origin point. Technique without mystification. Emotion without performance. The field's root signal is authentic emission in constrained circumstances.

lineages.name: Chicago Blues

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: urban tension, community gathering, resilience as communal act

lineages.cultural_context: Black migration north created new density. Electric amplification gave the Delta voice to a room. Chess Records, Muddy Waters, Howlin Wolf. The industrial city translated through the acoustic field.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Electrification without losing the origin signal. Technology in service of expression, not expression reduced to technology. The body still drives the machine.

lineages.name: Gospel / Soul Roots

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: collective transcendence, grief resolved into joy, community as medium

lineages.cultural_context: The sacred tradition carrying the same emotional material as the blues but oriented toward communal transformation. Mahalia Jackson, Sam Cooke crossing over. The tension between serving a community and being captured by the market.
lineages.kindpath_notes: The church as the original regenerative field holder. Community singing as distributed sovereignty. The voice as both individual and collective signal.

lineages.name: Early Rock and Roll

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: physical energy, transgression, youth claiming space

lineages.cultural_context: White market reception of Black artistic forms — a pattern of extraction that would define the industry for decades. Chuck Berry, Little Richard, Elvis. The original three-stage mechanism: priming through rhythm, prestige through novelty, identity capture through youth culture.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Note the mechanism at its origin: the form carries regenerative energy from its creators; the market captures the signal and redirects the value. Understanding this lineage means understanding the extraction pattern at the root.

lineages.name: British Invasion

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: romanticism, youth culture, studio as instrument

lineages.cultural_context: Blues tradition returned to America via Britain — transformed, whitened, amplified into a global commercial force. The Beatles, Rolling Stones. Studio became composition space. Production quality as artistic medium.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Second-order signal: the form that learned from the extractors and accelerated the institutional capture of Black creative traditions. Sonic evidence of how far a signal can travel from its origin.

lineages.name: Psychedelia

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: dissolution of ego, collective consciousness, sensory expansion, institutional questioning

lineages.cultural_context: Counter-cultural moment where the studio was weaponised against consensus reality. Anti-war, anti-institution. The field was briefly bent toward collective questioning of conditioning itself — then captured and packaged.
lineages.kindpath_notes: The moment the field articulated resistance to its own conditioning. High LSII signatures common — the pieces step outside the frames they build. Psychedelia as evidence that the conditioning can be seen and named from within a commercial form.

lineages.name: Funk

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: collective body sovereignty, joy as resistance, rhythm as liberation

lineages.cultural_context: James Brown, Sly Stone, Parliament-Funkadelic. The body as political instrument. Joy as refusal. Groove as communal technology. The highest groove_deviation scores in the recorded archive — the body leading, the grid following.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Reference point for authentic groove deviation. High groove_deviation_ms in a funk piece is the signal of healthy collective embodiment. Use as baseline for measuring what has been quantised out of later decades.

lineages.name: Early Electronic / Krautrock

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: machine meditation, forward motion without destination, industrial landscape as aesthetic

lineages.cultural_context: Can, Kraftwerk, Neu!, Tangerine Dream. Post-war German rejection of Anglo-American rock lineage. Technology as pure medium — no emotional pretension. The motorik rhythm: constant, non-emphatic forward motion. Foundation of everything that followed in electronic music.
lineages.kindpath_notes: The template for electronic music as a distinct field. Low groove_deviation is intentional here — the machine as honest about its nature. Contrast with music that machines human performance to remove groove_deviation as a manufacturing choice.

lineages.name: Disco

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: physical pleasure, liberation through dance, queer and Black club space as sanctuary

lineages.cultural_context: Emerged from Black, Latino, and LGBTQ+ club culture as a space of safety and joy. The 4-on-the-floor kick as body entrainment technology — used here for collective liberation, later appropriated as conditioning mechanism. The backlash (Disco Demolition Night) was explicit hostility.
lineages.kindpath_notes: The 4-on-the-floor pattern originated here as communal tool. Track this forward into house, techno, EDM — the same pattern, different directions of use. Disco's original context was syntropic: marginalised communities creating safety through sonic field.

lineages.name: Post-Punk / New Wave

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: alienation, intellectual rigour, body-held anxiety, critique of consumer culture

lineages.cultural_context: Wire, Joy Division, Gang of Four, Talking Heads. Punk's critique made more sophisticated. Machine and human held in tension. Often explicitly anti-commercial, anti-prestige. The body registering political unease as physical sensation.
lineages.kindpath_notes: High authenticity markers common — production intentionally avoids prestige signals. The tension_resolution_ratio often > 1 (unresolved). This is content that sits in the discomfort rather than manufacturing relief.

lineages.name: Early Hip-Hop

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: witness, community address, wordplay as sovereignty, collective memory as resource

lineages.cultural_context: Bronx South Bronx as origin: resourcefulness under economic devastation (the landlord burning buildings for insurance, the Cross Bronx Expressway destroying community). Technology (turntables, drum machines) as democratised instruments. Grandmaster Flash, Afrika Bambaataa. Technique belongs to everyone — the crate-digging ethic.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Crate-digging as the origin practice: finding the origin signal in recorded archives, isolating it, giving it back to the community. This module literally performs that function for any audio file.

lineages.name: House

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: collective trance, body as vehicle for community, utopian joy, queer and Black dance space

lineages.cultural_context: Chicago's Warehouse and Music Box clubs. Frankie Knuckles, Larry Heard. Black and queer communities creating their own field under hostile conditions. The machine serving the body rather than replacing it. The tempo entrains the heart.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Where the electronic tradition absorbed soul and created a new communal form. The four-on-the-floor here is in service of collective embodiment — the same pattern that corporate EDM would later deploy for extraction. Classify by context, not pattern alone.

lineages.name: Techno

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: machine consciousness, post-industrial identity, future as uncertain territory, collective endurance

lineages.cultural_context: Detroit: Juan Atkins, Derrick May, Kevin Saunderson. Post-industrial city where the machines replaced the workers who made them. Techno as processing that reality through the machines' own language. Music that acknowledged the post-work future before it arrived.
lineages.kindpath_notes: High creative_residue scores common — significant departure from any prior genre baseline. Techno's emotional honesty about industrial reality is a form of syntropy: naming what is, rather than papering over it with false hope.

lineages.name: Grunge / Alternative

production_era_signatures: dynamic contrast between verse and chorus
sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: alienation, anger as authenticity signal, rejection of performance, body-held generational anxiety

lineages.cultural_context: Generation X processing 1980s excess. Nirvana, Soundgarden, Pearl Jam. The dynamic arc (quiet verse → loud chorus) as the sonic structure of held and released tension. Commercial success arrived and then consumed the very authenticity it had been attracted to.
lineages.kindpath_notes: High LSII common: Kurt Cobain's late-song inversions are famous for a reason. The body registering what can't be said. Preserved dynamic range as authentic marker that commercial pressure later compressed away.

lineages.name: UK Rave / Jungle / D&B

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: physical urgency, community under pressure, multiculturalism as lived practice

lineages.cultural_context: UK multicultural communities in South London, Bristol, East London. Caribbean musical traditions meeting electronic production tools. The amen break — a 2-second drum solo from 1969 — became the shared raw material for an entire generation. Community in the warehouse, outside the approved music infrastructure.
lineages.kindpath_notes: The amen break is the most-sampled recording in history — nearly unrecognised by the creators. The pattern of origin signal extracted from its creators repeated. But jungle and D&B communities created something genuinely new from shared material — communal creative residue at a high level.

lineages.name: Trip-Hop

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: melancholy, interiority, urban isolation, contemplative withdrawal

lineages.cultural_context: Bristol (Massive Attack, Portishead, Tricky), London, Mo Wax. The deceleration of hip-hop into introspective space. Mourning the loss of the utopian rave moment. Vinyl as sacred archive material.
lineages.kindpath_notes: High LSII scores common in trip-hop — the genre is defined by late-song withdrawal and incompletion as artistic choice. High HNR in vocals (Portishead, Beth Gibbons) — the voice's tensions left unprocessed as an honest signal.

lineages.name: IDM

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: cognitive complexity as emotional register, machine interiority, non-linear time

lineages.cultural_context: Aphex Twin, Autechre, Boards of Canada. Pushing electronic music toward extreme compositional complexity and away from functional dance use. The label 'Intelligent' was contested — the implicit contrast problematic, the music genuine.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Highest harmonic_complexity and creative_residue scores in the reference library. IDM as sonic research — technique serving exploration rather than market function. The high complexity is often the authentic emission, not prestige signalling.

lineages.name: Minimal Techno

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: contemplative endurance, stripped ego, body-as-rhythm-machine, collective trance

lineages.cultural_context: Berlin post-reunification. Richie Hawtin, Robert Hood, Ricardo Villalobos. Berghain as institution. Reduction as aesthetic and ethical position — precision over complexity. Long sets, no lighting, no phone cameras. Temporary community through shared attention.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Low creative_residue by design — the minimal aesthetic is intentional. Manufacturing_score may read high due to production precision, but the context is authentic. Classification requires cultural context, not just signal analysis.

lineages.name: Psytrance

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: trance induction, collective dissolution, hypnotic repetition, consciousness as landscape

lineages.cultural_context: Goa beaches, Israeli electronic scene, global gathering culture. Music as ritual technology. The full-day festival format: music as environment rather than entertainment. Community through shared altered state.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Highest body_priming scores — the 4-on-the-floor at 145 BPM with persistent bass oscillation is explicit somatic entrainment. In context (outdoor gathering, community intention) this is communal. Extracted into commercial festival or playlist context, the same mechanics become extraction. Context determines direction.

lineages.name: UK Grime

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: urban witness, community address, anger as precise instrument, territorial pride

lineages.cultural_context: East London pirate radio, council estates. Dizzee Rascal, Skepta, Wiley. Young Black British communities creating expressive infrastructure outside and despite the official music industry. Music as community communication technology.
lineages.kindpath_notes: High authentic_emission scores in early grime. The pirate radio context meant no commercial mediation — direct community address. As grime entered the mainstream, the manufacturing_score rises and the authentic signal is progressively reconstructed for market consumption.

lineages.name: Post-Dubstep / Bass Music

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: interiority, urban grief held without resolution, the body as primary listener

lineages.cultural_context: Burial, Actress, The xx. Music that processed the post-financial-crisis condition — community dissolving, intimacy becoming harder, digital isolation alongside physical proximity. Bass frequencies as the honest emotional register of the body.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Burial's work carries one of the highest LSII records in the reference library. The late-song inversions are total — the last quarter of a Burial piece often deconstructs everything that came before. The body's late-stage signature is in the work.

lineages.name: Contemporary R&B

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: intimacy performed at scale, vulnerability as product, emotional labour made content

lineages.cultural_context: Frank Ocean, SZA, The Weeknd. The streaming era's compression of emotional range. Authentic-seeming vulnerability delivered through maximally processed signal. The market learned that the appearance of authenticity is more valuable than authenticity — the three-stage mechanism fully internalised.
lineages.kindpath_notes: High pitch_correction_likelihood + low performance_artifacts = classic manufacturing signal even when the emotional content is marketed as raw. The three-stage mechanism operating at peak efficiency. High manufacturing + low creative_residue is the diagnostic signature.

lineages.name: Hyperpop

sonic_characteristics.emotional_register: ironic sincerity, digital body, aestheticized suffering, post-ironic yearning

lineages.cultural_context: PC Music, 100 gecs, SOPHIE. Hypercompression and maximalism as both critique and embrace of late-stage consumer culture aesthetics. Online-native communities processing identity in digital environments. SOPHIE's work specifically carried deep authenticity about trans identity.
lineages.kindpath_notes: Complex classification case: manufactured aesthetic that carries genuine authentic emission for specific communities. The distortion and maximalism read as high manufacturing_score, but the creative_residue is often high too. The signal is doing something genuine even through its maximally processed surface.
